cpuResults.label: flatten + prepareParagraph, cold
cpuResults.desc: One cold 500-text batch; segment-metrics cache cleared per run (engine shaping caches under TextPainter cannot be cleared)

cpuResults.label: fresh TextPainter().layout(400)
cpuResults.desc: One 500-text batch, new painter per text

cpuResults.label: layoutPreparedLines over prepared batch
cpuResults.desc: Hot per-width relayout, widths cycled 200/250/300/350/400px, 200 repeats normalized

cpuResults.label: retained TextPainter.layout(w)
cpuResults.desc: Relayout at cycled widths on retained painters — the engine has no prepare/layout split, so this re-breaks from scratch (that asymmetry is the headline number)

frameResults.label: font load → pipeline → first frame
frameResults.desc: Cold start: Lato parse+register, GPU pipeline build, then the mount frame of one paragraph (build/raster from FrameTiming)

frameResults.label: first frame (bundle fonts preloaded)
frameResults.desc: Mount frame of one RichText paragraph; asset fonts are loaded by the engine at startup, so no comparable font-load split exists

frameResults.label: first mount of a full CJK corpus paragraph
frameResults.desc: zh-zhufu (9428 chars, thousands of unique glyphs): cold pays banding + full curves/rows texture re-upload on the gputext side

frameResults.label: identical remount (atlas + prepare cache warm)
frameResults.desc: zh-zhufu (9428 chars, thousands of unique glyphs): cold pays banding + full curves/rows texture re-upload on the gputext side

frameResults.label: first mount of a full CJK corpus paragraph
frameResults.desc: zh-zhufu (9428 chars, thousands of unique glyphs): cold pays banding + full curves/rows texture re-upload on the gputext side

frameResults.label: identical remount (atlas + prepare cache warm)
frameResults.desc: zh-zhufu (9428 chars, thousands of unique glyphs): cold pays banding + full curves/rows texture re-upload on the gputext side

frameResults.label: 30 static paragraphs, idle vsync
frameResults.desc: Floor/sanity: nothing changes; both sides should build ~0. GPUText steady-state paint is a cached-image blit — pair with the dynamic scenarios below for a fair picture.

frameResults.label: 30 static paragraphs, idle vsync
frameResults.desc: Floor/sanity: nothing changes; both sides should build ~0. GPUText steady-state paint is a cached-image blit — pair with the dynamic scenarios below for a fair picture.

frameResults.label: per-frame color toggle, 30 paragraphs
frameResults.desc: RenderComparison.paint each frame: gputext re-emits instances and re-renders its GPU surface; RichText re-records. The honest "gputext is not just a blit" case.

frameResults.label: per-frame color toggle, 30 paragraphs
frameResults.desc: RenderComparison.paint each frame: gputext re-emits instances and re-renders its GPU surface; RichText re-records. The honest "gputext is not just a blit" case.

frameResults.label: per-frame text change, 12 paragraphs
frameResults.desc: RenderComparison.layout each frame: full flatten+prepare+break both sides; every prepare-cache lookup misses (unique keys).

frameResults.label: per-frame text change, 12 paragraphs
frameResults.desc: RenderComparison.layout each frame: full flatten+prepare+break both sides; every prepare-cache lookup misses (unique keys).

frameResults.label: animated wrap width, 20 paragraphs
frameResults.desc: Width 240↔420 px sinusoid: gputext re-breaks from cached prepares (the prepare/layout split); RichText relays out from scratch. Resize-heal re-renders are counted in surfaceRenders.

frameResults.label: animated wrap width, 20 paragraphs
frameResults.desc: Width 240↔420 px sinusoid: gputext re-breaks from cached prepares (the prepare/layout split); RichText relays out from scratch. Resize-heal re-renders are counted in surfaceRenders.

frameResults.label: long-document scroll, full Gatsby corpus
frameResults.desc: Constant 24 px/frame with direction reversal: steady-state blit vs engine repaint, plus viewport-entry cold prepares. This is gputext's best case by construction.

frameResults.label: long-document scroll, full Gatsby corpus
frameResults.desc: Constant 24 px/frame with direction reversal: steady-state blit vs engine repaint, plus viewport-entry cold prepares. This is gputext's best case by construction.

frameResults.label: Transform.scale zoom 1→8→1
frameResults.desc: Exponential zoom sweep, transformAdaptive on: gputext re-renders crisp at each quantized 1.25× step (surfaceRenders ≈ steps crossed); RichText rasters once and scales (blurry — see vis.zoom_4x for the quality side).

frameResults.label: Transform.scale zoom 1→8→1
frameResults.desc: Exponential zoom sweep, transformAdaptive on: gputext re-renders crisp at each quantized 1.25× step (surfaceRenders ≈ steps crossed); RichText rasters once and scales (blurry — see vis.zoom_4x for the quality side).

frameResults.label: InteractiveViewer step zoom ×1.25
frameResults.desc: Programmatic TransformationController sweep, 6-frame dwell per 1.25× step (up to ~6×, back down); gputext pass wires scaleHint so re-render triggers cross the RepaintBoundary.

frameResults.label: InteractiveViewer step zoom ×1.25
frameResults.desc: Programmatic TransformationController sweep, 6-frame dwell per 1.25× step (up to ~6×, back down); gputext pass wires scaleHint so re-render triggers cross the RepaintBoundary.

frameResults.label: 240 unique-text cells, mount + steady
frameResults.desc: Many-widget overhead: 240 small paragraphs all visible; measurement includes the mount frame.

frameResults.label: 240 unique-text cells, mount + steady
frameResults.desc: Many-widget overhead: 240 small paragraphs all visible; measurement includes the mount frame.

frameResults.label: 240 identical-text cells, mount + steady
frameResults.desc: Shared prepare-cache effect: identical spans should flatten+prepare once and hit the cache ~239 times (gputext pass).

frameResults.label: 240 identical-text cells, mount + steady
frameResults.desc: Shared prepare-cache effect: identical spans should flatten+prepare once and hit the cache ~239 times (gputext pass).

frameResults.label: justified reflow, 12 paragraphs
frameResults.desc: reflow_width driver with TextAlign.justify (greedy breaks both sides).

frameResults.label: justified reflow, 12 paragraphs
frameResults.desc: reflow_width driver with TextAlign.justify (greedy breaks both sides).

frameResults.label: Knuth–Plass justified reflow, 12 paragraphs
frameResults.desc: Same driver with the TeX-style optimal-fit breaker; Flutter has no counterpart (compare against frame.justify).

frameResults.label: variable-font wght animation
frameResults.desc: GoogleSansFlex wght 100↔1000 in 64 quantized steps: each new coordinate bands fresh outlines into the atlas (variants never evict — see mem.varfont_growth).

frameResults.label: variable-font wght animation
frameResults.desc: GoogleSansFlex wght 100↔1000 in 64 quantized steps: each new coordinate bands fresh outlines into the atlas (variants never evict — see mem.varfont_growth).

frameResults.label: mixed emoji/CJK/URL text, per-frame change
frameResults.desc: Hybrid path: emoji clusters and uncovered characters expand into nested native Text WidgetSpans, which also disables the prepare cache. Not comparable with pure rows.

frameResults.label: mixed emoji/CJK/URL text, per-frame change
frameResults.desc: Hybrid path: emoji clusters and uncovered characters expand into nested native Text WidgetSpans, which also disables the prepare cache. Not comparable with pure rows.

frameResults.label: 12 paragraphs × 8 WidgetSpans, per-frame change
frameResults.desc: Cache-disabled worst case: inline children force a fresh flatten+prepare every build (gputext pass).

frameResults.label: 12 paragraphs × 8 WidgetSpans, per-frame change
frameResults.desc: Cache-disabled worst case: inline children force a fresh flatten+prepare every build (gputext pass).

memoryResults.desc: atlas cost of banding the zh corpus glyph set
atlas growth across the wght animation (variants never evict)
200 unique paragraphs laid out and painted (single-child scroll view paints the full column). RichText row reports RSS only — engine paragraph memory is opaque from Dart.
